result:
I’d make “basic needs are guaranteed” a global default: everyone automatically has enough food, clean water, safe shelter, essential healthcare (including mental health), and basic education—no eligibility maze, no stigma, no precariousness.

That one change cascades into a lot: less desperation-driven crime and exploitation, better childhood outcomes, more stable democracies, more willingness to take productive risks (start businesses, leave abusive jobs/relationships), and a huge drop in preventable suffering. It wouldn’t solve everything, but it would raise the floor for nearly every other problem we try to tackle.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
